ead.archdesc:
======= RMA01964Cornell University ceramics, glassware, and metalware collection41-7-1964Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
June 20182B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:

This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University ceramics, glassware, and metalware collection
Cornell University
41-7-1964
19.1 cubic feet. 19.1 cubic feet.
1893-2003. The Cornell University Ceramics, Glassware, and Metalware collection consists of items commissioned by the University, fraternities and societies, and departments. Materials include correspondence concerning Cornell Wedgwood china; full sets of Cornell Plates, made by Wedgwood, in Mulberry and blue, with twelve different designs, depicting Cornell scenes and buildings including the Crescent, Sage Chapel, Baker Laboratory, Myron Taylor Hall, Goldwin Smith Hall, Balch Halls, the Clock Tower, the War Memorial, McGraw Hall, Willard Straight Hall, the Ezra Cornell Statue, and Sibley Dome. Also includes a full set of Wedgwood mulberry and blue cups and saucers and an Incomplete set of green saucers and cups. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 27 - folder 1 ======= 27 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2075344
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
June 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, June 2018
Date modified:
Jude Corina, June 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The original set of Cornell plates was created in 1932, based on a design created by E. Stewart Williams (Class of 1933) for the Baird Prize "Place Plate" artwork competition for Design 112, February 1932. The winning design was used by the Wedgwood Company for a set of twelve plates depicting Cornell scenes. Originally created in mulberry, copper green, and Staffordshire blue, they were advertised in the Cornell Alumni News and sold through the Cornell Alumni Corporation. They continued to be advertised and sold through at least 1943. In 1950, they were re-issued.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The Cornell University Ceramics, Glassware, and Metalware collection consists of items commissioned by the University, fraternities and societies, and departments. Materials include correspondence concerning Cornell Wedgwood china; full sets of Cornell Plates, made by Wedgwood, in Mulberry and blue, with twelve different designs, depicting Cornell scenes and buildings including the Crescent, Sage Chapel, Baker Laboratory, Myron Taylor Hall, Goldwin Smith Hall, Balch Halls, the Clock Tower, the War Memorial, McGraw Hall, Willard Straight Hall, the Ezra Cornell Statue, and Sibley Dome. Also includes a full set of Wedgwood mulberry and blue cups and saucers and an Incomplete set of green saucers and cups.
The collection also contains mugs, cups, and glasses from different class years, reunions, sororities, fraternities, and departments, as well as commemorative plates.
Also, correspondence concerning Cornell Wedgwood china.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Wedgwood Plates, Cups, and Saucers
Series II. Cornell Mugs, Cups, and Glasses
Series III. Cornell Plates (non-Wedgwood)
Cite As:
Cornell University. Alumni Association. Cornell Plates. Records, #41-7-1964. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also Cornell University Centennial Celebration records, #47-4-798 for glasses from this event.
Wedgwood ware. Greek letter societies -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Williams, Stewart, 1909-2005. Cornell Crew (Rowing team) Cornell University. Alembic Society Cornell University -- : Athletics. Cornell University. Class of 1912 Cornell University. Class of 1920 Cornell University. Class of 1936 Cornell University. Class of 1939 Cornell University. Class of 1940 Cornell University. Class of 1944 Cornell University. Class of 1945 Cornell University. Class of 1983 Cornell University. Class of 1987 Cornell University -- : Societies, etc. Cosmopolitan Club (Cornell University) Cornell Law School Sigma Alpha Mu Tau Delta Eta Pi Lambda Phi
